plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and comply with the mandate.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and stay within the single name limit.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for income stability and broad market exposure.
Buy NOW at 5% as a high-quality, strong scanner setup to diversify slightly.
This plan aims for capital preservation by reducing overall risk and maintaining a cash buffer.
The portfolio will be less concentrated, with lower leverage and a focus on stable assets.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETF risk and meet mandate limits.
Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and align with the single name limit.
Reduce QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for a trim by the scanner.
Increase QQQ to 25% for balanced broad market exposure.
Buy ANET and TXN at 7% each, adding quality tech with strong risk/reward setups.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, balancing upside potential with downside protection.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and adhere to the single name limit.
Reduce QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for a trim by the scanner.
Buy NOW, ANET, and NVDU at 10% each, leaning into high-scoring scanner setups.
This plan utilizes available leverage and concentrates in strong, working setups.
A smaller cash buffer is maintained, with strict stops for managing increased risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It involves higher concentration and leverage, increasing both upside potential and drawdown risk. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and significant losses can occur rapidly if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Sell all SOXL, SPAL, SMH, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta exposure.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with the mandate.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize monthly distributions and income focus.
Add QQQ to 15% for stable broad market exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown over growth.
A significant portion of the book will be in income-generating and broad index ETFs.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, focusing on steady monthly distributions. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged and high-beta holdings. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
